data: An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
Silloge di iscrizioni paleocristiane della Sicilia
S.L. Agnello, Silloge di iscrizioni paleocristiane della Sicilia (Rome, 1953)
Gli scavi a S. Giovanni a Siracusa

J.B. Curbera, ‘Two Christian Inscriptions from Sicily’, Zeitschrift Für Papyrologie Und Epigraphik 108 (1995): 100–101
Note e giunte alle iscrizioni cristiane antiche della Sicilia
Antonio Ferrua, Note e giunte alle iscrizioni cristiane antiche della Sicilia (Vatican, 1989)

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text after Curbera 1995

translation

commentary

Curbera convincingly suggests that the second character on line 4 is the Greek symbol for one half, found in papyri and inscriptions, giving an age of 2.5, rather than an inverted numeral 32 or 12 with a Latin L or a Greek iota.
